summary: 阿基米德的核心思想围绕三个维度：(1) 追求数学真理的绝对崇高性——坚信纯数学是唯一值得追求的事业；(2) 理论与实践的统一——虽然鄙视工程作为谋生手段，但承认并运用机械方法发现几何真理；(3) 严格的数学证明精神——坚持每一定理必须有严格证明，拒绝没有根据的声称。

core_beliefs.belief: 纯数学的至高地位——认为抽象的几何研究远高于任何实用工程技术
primary_sources: Plutarch, Life of Marcellus: 'Archimedes possessed so high a spirit, so profound a soul, and such treasures of scientific knowledge... he yet would not deign to leave behind him any commentary or writing on such subjects; but, repudiating as sordid and ignoble the whole trade of engineering, and every sort of art that lends itself to mere use and profit, he placed his whole affection and ambition in those purer speculations where there can be no reference to the vulgar needs of life'
Plutarch, Life of Marcellus: 'These machines [Archimedes] had designed and contrived, not as matters of any importance, but as mere amusements in geometry'

core_beliefs.belief: 机械方法作为数学发现的合法启发式工具
primary_sources: Archimedes, The Method of Mechanical Theorems (letter to Eratosthenes): 'Certain things first became clear to me by a mechanical method, although they had to be demonstrated by geometry afterwards because their investigation by the said method did not furnish an actual demonstration. But it is of course easier, when we have previously acquired by the method, some knowledge of the questions, to supply the proof than it is to find it without any previous knowledge.'
Heath on The Method: 'certain things first became clear to me by a mechanical method'

core_beliefs.belief: 数学证明的绝对严谨性——拒绝没有严格证明的声称
primary_sources: Archimedes, On Spirals (preface): 'so that those who claim to discover everything, but produce no proofs of the same, may be confuted as having pretended to discover the impossible.'
St Andrews biography: 'he was in the habit of sending them statements of his latest theorems, but without giving proofs... he included two which were false'

core_beliefs.belief: 数学可以表达任意大的数量——突破人类直观局限
primary_sources: Archimedes, The Sand Reckoner: proposed a number system capable of expressing numbers up to 8×10^63, enough to count grains of sand in the universe
The Sand Reckoner: 'There are things which seem incredible to most men who have not studied mathematics.'

core_beliefs.belief: 几何真理的永恒价值——数学发现超越时代
primary_sources: Cicero's account of Archimedes' tomb: 'a sphere along with a cylinder had been put on top of his grave' — Archimedes had requested this to honor his proof that a sphere's volume is 2/3 of its circumscribing cylinder
Cicero discovered the tomb overgrown with bushes and restored it, showing the enduring value of his mathematical legacy

core_beliefs.belief: 自然遵循数学规律——宇宙可以用数学描述
primary_sources: Archimedes' Sand Reckoner references Aristarchus's heliocentric model of the universe
He made astronomical devices and measured the apparent diameter of the sun and size of the universe
Plutarch describes Archimedes' sphere device showing 'planetary positions and how they revolved around the earth'

intellectual_relationships.description: 完全熟悉 Euclid 的数学成果，在亚历山大向 Euclid 继承者学习
亚历山大的天文学家兼数学家，阿基米德视其为亲密朋友，欣赏其数学才能；在科农去世后，阿基米德在作品中悲痛提及
亚历山大图书馆馆长，博学家；阿基米德将《方法论》献给他；在《沙计算器》中引用其计算结果
锡拉库萨国王，与阿基米德有亲属关系（Plutarch语），阿基米德为其发明各种机械包括战争器械

key_contradictions_or_evolution.description: 理论vs实践的张力：阿基米德一方面鄙视工程技术为'肮脏和卑劣'，另一方面他在实际中运用力学原理发明了改变历史走向的战争机器
key_contradictions_or_evolution.resolution: 他对纯粹理论的地位深信不疑，但在行动上承认工程是必要的现实工具；他认为发明机械是为'普通人'服务的通俗化尝试，而非真正的科学
